title: You Dont Have To Live Like A Referee

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening of episode
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer catchphrase as cold open
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated into character - economical and quotable but not original

jokes.text: Hit the dirt, squirt!
jokes.setup: Educational Lincoln-Douglas debate recreation
jokes.punchline: Students throwing things at historical figures
jokes.explanation: Simple visual comedy of kids ruining educational presentation - competent but predictable

jokes.text: Get a room! / I shared beds with men all the time. It was a common practice. / Ooh, common practice.
jokes.setup: Student yelling inappropriate comment at historical debate
jokes.punchline: Lincoln's innocent historical explanation gets mocked
jokes.explanation: Good layered joke - innocent historical fact meets modern innuendo

jokes.text: Why are you concussing yourself?
jokes.setup: Actor being pelted by objects
jokes.punchline: Formal language describing obvious physical abuse
jokes.explanation: Great word choice - 'concussing yourself' is unexpectedly formal and clinical for the situation

jokes.text: Guess what, I also play Frankenstein!
jokes.setup: Educational presentation falling apart
jokes.punchline: Actor desperately plugging other gigs while being attacked
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd - the desperation and poor timing of self-promotion during chaos is perfectly executed

jokes.text: I have seven other principals, and I've never yelled their names, not even once.
jokes.setup: Chalmers yelling 'Skinner!'
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of his unique antagonistic relationship with Skinner
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor that acknowledges the running gag while adding new dimension

jokes.text: We had to rent out our multipurpose room to a Japanese-American social club. / Tanpatsu!
jokes.setup: School financial problems explanation
jokes.punchline: Random Japanese exclamation in background
jokes.explanation: Mild background gag - adds texture but not particularly clever

jokes.text: What about po' boys? / Sorry, Nelson. Poor boys such as yourself will go hungry.
jokes.setup: Nelson asking about po' boy sandwiches
jokes.punchline: Chalmers taking it literally about Nelson's economic status
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both cruel and perfectly in character for Chalmers' oblivious authority

jokes.text: What makes you a hero? / I don't eat as much as I did!
jokes.setup: Question about heroism to weight loss spokesperson
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic answer reducing heroism to basic self-control
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of hero concept - economical but not particularly innovative

jokes.text: My hero is my mom's boyfriend who took me to that movie when I was three. I call him Uncle Rob because... he robbed us!
jokes.setup: Sweet story about family figure taking child to movie
jokes.punchline: Revelation that 'Uncle' nickname comes from literal robbery
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect setup/payoff structure - dark humor executed skillfully

jokes.text: Okay, calm down. Maybe he's talking about Margaret Mead.
jokes.setup: Martin beginning elaborate introduction of his hero
jokes.punchline: Lisa hoping for different famous woman scientist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - intellectual hope mixed with competitive anxiety.

jokes.text: Everyone does that with good dads. But with our Dad, it's like climbing Mount Everest. You either plant your flag at the top of the world or get frozen in agony forever. Either way, hell of a show.
jokes.setup: Lisa considering making speech about Homer
jokes.punchline: Bart's metaphor about the extreme risk/reward of praising Homer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor perfectly capturing Homer's unpredictable nature - excellent character insight

jokes.text: I'm allowed to eat these!
jokes.setup: Homer at school event
jokes.punchline: Homer excited about permission to eat, like a child
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession - perfectly in character but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Loser. / Pathetic. / He makes me sick.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing her unheroic dad
jokes.punchline: Students' harsh running commentary
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe humor - functional but predictable audience cruelty

jokes.text: The trick is: wait several weeks.
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Homer's gentle Band-Aid removal
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy 'technique' revealed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - taking credit for kindness when it's actually neglect, delivered with characteristic obliviousness

jokes.text: In the history of soccer, he is the only parent to ever take sides against his own kid
jokes.setup: Lisa praising Homer's referee integrity
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about soccer history based on one incident
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration humor - Lisa's hyperbolic praise for Homer's basic fairness

jokes.text: Yes, which means that nobody wins. The money goes back in the school general fund.
jokes.setup: Announcement of tie in contest
jokes.punchline: School keeping prize money under bureaucratic pretense
jokes.explanation: Cynical but believable school administration behavior - competent institutional satire

jokes.text: Behind a paywall.
jokes.setup: School will put speeches online
jokes.punchline: Even student speeches require payment
jokes.explanation: Topical internet culture joke - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Where did my lawnmower go?
jokes.setup: Homer singing heroic song
jokes.punchline: Random domestic concern interrupting epic moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's mind wandering to mundane concerns during supposedly triumphant moment

jokes.text: There's sausage being made?! Where?!
jokes.setup: Lisa using 'sausage being made' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Homer taking it literally and getting excited about food
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession trumping comprehension - perfectly executed character moment

jokes.text: What you Americans call 'soccer.' / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Executive mentions World Cup football
jokes.punchline: Homer's disappointment when clarified as soccer
jokes.explanation: Standard American/international sports confusion joke with good Homer reaction

jokes.text: That's the thing the guys at the dry cleaners get so excited about every four years.
jokes.setup: FIFA executive describing World Cup importance
jokes.punchline: Homer's limited cultural awareness of soccer fandom
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about American disconnection from soccer culture

jokes.text: No, no, I mean it spread like wildfire. Much better.
jokes.setup: Executive saying video went viral
jokes.punchline: His preference for older metaphor
jokes.explanation: Mild generational humor. Works but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: No, no, I mean it spread like wildfire. / Much better.
jokes.setup: Executive uses 'viral' to describe video spread
jokes.punchline: Homer preferring literal disease metaphor
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay about Homer's discomfort with modern terminology

jokes.text: Yes, I will take good care of your wife. / Wait, what-what does that mean?
jokes.setup: Corrupt executive being arrested
jokes.punchline: Ominous statement about his wife's 'care'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corruption and implied adultery - competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Well, no one has ever questioned my professionalism. Except at my profession.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming professional integrity
jokes.punchline: Admission that his actual job questions his professionalism
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware Homer logic - perfectly structured contradiction revealing his work incompetence

jokes.text: The 2008 Super Bowl? / Oh, I wish. Eli Manning's pass to David Tyree.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about greatest sporting event
jokes.punchline: Lisa's wistful reference to specific football play
jokes.explanation: Specific sports reference for fans but not particularly funny construction

jokes.text: Thanks, book!
jokes.setup: Homer reading soccer ball instructions
jokes.punchline: Homer thanking the instruction manual as if it were a person
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer anthropomorphizing inanimate objects. Simple but character-consistent.

jokes.text: My TV didn't work!
jokes.setup: Homer confused why Lisa switched seats
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical reason for seat switch
jokes.explanation: Simple practical deflation of dramatic moment - competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: All right, just for that attitude, turbulence.
jokes.setup: Argument about learning Portuguese on plane
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant threatening to control weather as punishment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - flight attendant claiming supernatural powers over weather

jokes.text: Got to hurry. I've got 998 square miles to rob!
jokes.setup: Luxembourg's entire nation attending World Cup
jokes.punchline: Joke about country being so small one person could rob it all
jokes.explanation: Good geographical humor with specific square mileage adding credibility

jokes.text: How did he get those shorts on over that arse? / Rumor has it, they employed a team of pullers.
jokes.setup: Question about Homer's referee outfit logistics
jokes.punchline: Absurdly detailed explanation of dressing process
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor about Homer's size with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Andres Cantor, you know that is incorrect usage. / I have no control...! / This job has taken its toll...!
jokes.setup: Famous soccer announcer doing his signature 'GOAL' call
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his compulsive announcing style
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about famous announcer's signature style - good for soccer fans

jokes.text: The only thing that keeps me from living here is that fish that swims up your pee stream. That is a deal breaker.
jokes.setup: Homer appreciating Brazil
jokes.punchline: Specific biological fear preventing residence
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - specific unusual fear trumping all other considerations

jokes.text: I still can't believe the yellow and red cards don't stand for mustard and ketchup.
jokes.setup: Discussion of soccer officiating
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-based interpretation of referee cards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food obsession logic applied to sports rules

jokes.text: I still can't believe the yellow and red cards don't stand for mustard and ketchup.
jokes.setup: Homer being congratulated on officiating
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-based misunderstanding of soccer rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic. Food association with everything is peak character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, what other ways besides guns and money? / We have two ways. Two very good ways.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about corruption methods
jokes.punchline: Gangster's matter-of-fact confirmation of limited but effective toolkit
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor about criminal simplicity

jokes.text: Oh. How fresh? / Oh!
jokes.setup: Description of 'fresh' pork sandwich bribe
jokes.punchline: Homer's horrified reaction to actual freshness
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about food freshness being too fresh

jokes.text: You Americans, you really throw your $7.99s around.
jokes.setup: Marge's app spending habits
jokes.punchline: Foreign perspective on American casual spending
jokes.explanation: Good cultural observation about American spending habits from outside perspective.

jokes.text: I did not pay $7.99 for an app so I could use the English option.
jokes.setup: Brazilian suggesting Marge use English option
jokes.punchline: Marge's stubborn justification for language learning app purchase
jokes.explanation: Relatable modern technology frustration with specific price point adding authenticity

jokes.text: And it better be devastating.
jokes.setup: Bart promising to reveal something about Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer actively wanting to be hurt
jokes.explanation: Good Homer masochism. Shows his weird psychology about wanting dramatic revelation.

jokes.text: But knowing that Lisa chose me as her hero, without ever considering any other hero, keeps me strong.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his moral strength source
jokes.punchline: Dramatic irony since audience knows Lisa had another hero first
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony building toward revelation about Lisa's deception

jokes.text: I'm just examining what kind of person I am and whether I should destroy your happiness forever. / Eh, why not?
jokes.setup: Bart considering whether to tell Homer the truth
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual decision to cause emotional damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bart characterization - perfectly casual cruelty delivered with philosophical consideration

jokes.text: And now I'm a man in pain in the naughtiest city since San Francisco turned all nerdy.
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional devastation in Brazil
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural commentary about San Francisco's transformation
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural observation about tech industry's impact on San Francisco

jokes.text: But first I'm gonna eat off someone else's room service tray that was left in the hall.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring intent to drink away pain
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing food scavenging before emotional breakdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - food obsession trumps even emotional crisis

jokes.text: It's okay. I'm an American.
jokes.setup: Hotel staff questioning Homer eating hall food
jokes.punchline: Homer using nationality as excuse for bad behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple stereotype humor about American tourists' behavior expectations

jokes.text: But beware: Superman was never less fun.
jokes.setup: Gangster offering pirated Man of Steel movie
jokes.punchline: Accurate criticism of Superman movie quality
jokes.explanation: Topical movie criticism joke - economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Nazis! / Nazi harborers! / Guys, you're both right.
jokes.setup: Brazil vs Germany World Cup setup
jokes.punchline: Escalating Nazi accusations and diplomatic resolution
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor with diplomatic punchline - competent but predictable

jokes.text: If I bet it, I could double it. / But on who? / You know that Brazil is going to win. / Oh, thanks for telling me! I was gonna tape it.
jokes.setup: Gangster explaining fixed game to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it like TV spoiler instead of corruption
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - missing corruption implications for mundane TV concern

jokes.text: Well, if we're gonna be brutally honest, that drawing you made of me when you were three was far from a perfect likeness. / You put it on the fridge! / It was pity-fridged!
jokes.setup: Homer and Lisa exchanging honest criticisms
jokes.punchline: Homer's retroactive dismissal of childhood art display
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating honesty sequence with perfect family dynamic - 'pity-fridged' is brilliantly cruel and quotable

jokes.text: If I die, I'll be doing the thing I love the most: trying not to get killed.
jokes.setup: Homer facing potential death from gangsters
jokes.punchline: Philosophical observation about survival instinct
jokes.explanation: Clever philosophical paradox delivered with Homer's characteristic logic - well-structured and quotable

jokes.text: ♪ Olé... ♪ / ♪ Olé, olé, olé... ♪
jokes.setup: Brazil loses World Cup final
jokes.punchline: Dejected, slow Brazilian chanting after defeat
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about sports disappointment - competent but unremarkable

jokes.text: Well, at least I'll die the American way. In a foreign country wearing short pants.
jokes.setup: Homer facing death from Brazilian gangsters
jokes.punchline: Cultural observation about American tourist stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about American tourist behavior and appearance abroad

jokes.text: How's she doing that? / I don't know, but she's my new hero. / Yeah, I was a little surprised I wasn't considered before.
jokes.setup: Marge speaking Portuguese to save Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact comment about not being considered heroic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Marge with understated delivery about her unrecognized contributions

jokes.text: On a 15-hour flight!
jokes.setup: Dismissing Marge's airplane seat switch as minor
jokes.punchline: Mother emphasizing the extreme duration makes it significant
jokes.explanation: Callback to airplane seat switch with emphasis on flight duration as significant factor

jokes.text: On a 15-hour flight! You are free to go.
jokes.setup: Marge's airplane seat switch being described as heroic
jokes.punchline: Minor inconvenience treated as massive sacrifice worthy of debt forgiveness
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation. Minor airline inconvenience treated as heroic sacrifice.

jokes.text: The best thing is I can get locally-sourced monkey meat.
jokes.setup: Homer admiring the Amazon rainforest
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-focused appreciation of pristine nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment. Reduces natural wonder to food opportunity with perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Shh!
jokes.setup: End credits
jokes.punchline: Someone shushing during credits
jokes.explanation: Minor end credits gag. Very simple and not particularly memorable.

tmdb_overview: Homer is recruited as a World Cup referee after Lisa extols his integrity on the youth soccer field in a Springfield Elementary speech contest. But when the Simpsons travel to Brazil (again), Homer finds his honesty tested by one of South America's slickest gangsters in the soccer match-bribing business.
